differential_diagnoses: Severe insulin resistance syndrome (e.g., Donohue syndrome or Rabson-Mendenhall syndrome)
Factitious hyperglycemia (insulin non-adherence or surreptitious injection)
Lipodystrophy associated with autoimmune connective tissue disease
next_steps: Test for serum insulin-binding antibodies to screen for insulin autoimmune syndrome
Perform controlled insulin administration under strict observation to rule out factitious disorder/medication nonadherence
Evaluate for specific autoantibodies related to Type B insulin resistance (e.g., anti-insulin receptor antibodies)
Re-evaluate for underlying autoimmune or hematologic disorders given positive ANA, vitiligo, and marrow findings
Discuss candidacy for leptin replacement therapy (metreleptin) given the confirmed clinical phenotype and undetectable leptin levels
Consult endocrinology, rheumatology, and psychiatry for multidisciplinary management
